questions.stem: A woman is typing on a laptop computer.
questions.explanation: Focus on the action being performed: 'typing' on a laptop.

options.text: A woman is typing on a laptop.
A woman is sleeping on a couch.
A woman is cooking in a kitchen.
A woman is driving a car.

questions.stem: Several people are seated around a conference table.
questions.explanation: The key is 'seated around' which describes the arrangement.

options.text: People are running in a park.
People are gathered around a meeting table.
People are swimming in a pool.

questions.stem: A chef is preparing food in a commercial kitchen.
questions.explanation: Identify the occupation and action: chef + preparing food.

options.text: A chef is cooking in a restaurant kitchen.
A cleaner is mopping the floor.

questions.stem: Construction workers are wearing safety helmets on a building site.
questions.explanation: Match the subjects (construction workers) with their action/state.

options.text: Workers are wearing protective gear at a construction site.
Students are studying in a library.
Athletes are competing in a stadium.
Tourists are taking photos at a museum.

questions.stem: Packages are stacked on shelves in a warehouse.
questions.explanation: Focus on the passive state: packages 'are stacked' on shelves.

options.text: Boxes are arranged on storage shelves.
Customers are shopping in a store.

questions.stem: The man is arranging goods in a warehouse.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Stock boy image description.

options.text: The man is arranging goods in a warehouse.
The man is reading a newspaper.
The man is talking on the phone.

questions.stem: The children are participating in the lesson.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Classroom scene.

options.text: The children are participating in the lesson.

questions.stem: They are discussing the architectural plans.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Architects at work.

options.text: They are discussing the architectural plans.

questions.stem: The security guard is searching the man.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Security checkpoint.

options.text: The security guard is searching the man.

questions.stem: The ice cream cones are in rows.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Ice cream display.

options.text: The ice cream cones are in rows.

questions.stem: The barges are tied to posts.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Venice canal scene.

options.text: People are swimming in the water.
The barges are tied to posts.

questions.stem: The men are working on a solar panel.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Solar panel installation.

options.text: The men are working on a solar panel.

questions.stem: The airport lounge is not busy.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Airport waiting area.

options.text: The airport is crowded with passengers.
The airport lounge is not busy.
People are running to catch flights.

questions.stem: The farmer is holding the door open.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Farmer and tractor scene.

options.text: The farmer is holding the door open.
The farmer is milking a cow.
The farmer is riding a horse.

questions.stem: The man is carrying a box.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Supermarket scene.

options.text: The man is pushing a cart.
The man is eating an apple.
The man is reading a magazine.
The man is carrying a box.

questions.stem: The meeting is not going well.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Office meeting situation.

options.text: The meeting is not going well.

questions.stem: The people are in a laboratory.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Lab workers.

options.text: The people are in a restaurant.
The people are at a concert.
The people are in a laboratory.
The people are at a beach.

questions.stem: The mechanic is checking the computer data.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Auto repair shop.

options.text: The mechanic is washing the car.
The mechanic is changing a tire.
The mechanic is painting the car.
The mechanic is checking the computer data.

questions.stem: The doctor is talking with the patient.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Medical consultation.

options.text: The doctor is talking with the patient.
The doctor is reading a book.

questions.stem: The woman is handing the passenger his ticket.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Airline counter scene.

options.text: The woman is cleaning the counter.
The woman is handing the passenger his ticket.
The woman is making an announcement.

questions.stem: The container ship is in port.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Shipping port scene.

options.text: The ship is sailing on the ocean.
The container ship is in port.

questions.stem: The workers are above the ground.
questions.explanation: Real TOEIC Part 1 - Construction workers on scaffolding.

options.text: The workers are above the ground.
The workers are leaving the site.

questions.stem: Where is the meeting room?
questions.explanation: 'Where' questions require location answers.

questions.stem: Who is responsible for the project?
questions.explanation: 'Who' questions require a person as the answer.

questions.stem: How long will the repair take?
questions.explanation: 'How long' asks for duration.

questions.stem: Would you like coffee or tea?
questions.explanation: An 'or' question is answered by choosing one option.

questions.stem: Shouldn't we confirm the reservation?
questions.explanation: Negative questions suggest agreement or an explanation.

options.text: You're right, I'll call them now.

questions.stem: Have you had a chance to review the proposal?
questions.explanation: A polite inquiry expects a status update.

options.text: Not yet, but I'll do it this afternoon.
The proposal is on your desk.
Yes, I sent it last week.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?
questions.explanation: Identify the main topic from context clues.

questions.stem: What does the woman suggest?
questions.explanation: Listen for suggestion language like 'Why don't we' or 'How about'.

questions.stem: What problem does the man mention?
questions.explanation: Identify the issue or obstacle stated by the speaker.

options.text: The supplier is out of stock.
The system is down for maintenance.

questions.stem: What will the woman probably do next?
questions.explanation: Listen for future action cues like 'I'll' or 'Let me'.

options.text: Send an email to the team

questions.stem: Why does the man say 'That's exactly what I was thinking'?
questions.explanation: Understand implied meaning: agreeing with a suggestion.

options.text: To express agreement with the proposal
To indicate he has finished his work

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the announcement?
questions.explanation: Identify why the speaker is giving this talk.

questions.stem: According to the speaker, what should employees do by Friday?
questions.explanation: Listen for specific deadline instructions.

questions.stem: Where will the event take place?
questions.explanation: Listen for location words and venue names.

questions.stem: What benefit is mentioned for attendees?
questions.explanation: Identify positive outcomes or incentives.

questions.stem: Who is the intended audience for this message?
questions.explanation: Determine who the speaker is addressing.

questions.stem: The flight arrives ___ Tokyo in three hours.
questions.explanation: Use 'in' for arriving in a city/country.

questions.stem: He is excited about the new promotion and looking forward to ___ more responsibilities.
questions.explanation: 'Taking on' means to accept or acquire new duties.

questions.stem: My line manager wants the meeting ___ immediately.
questions.explanation: Past participle 'arranged' follows 'want + object'.

questions.stem: The software developers ___ investigated the latest problem.
questions.explanation: 'Have already' indicates completed action in present perfect.

questions.stem: While the stock ___ the staff worked in the evenings.
questions.explanation: Past continuous passive: 'was being checked'.

questions.stem: ___ the end of year results were published, the managers got their bonuses.
questions.explanation: 'When' indicates the time relationship.

questions.stem: All the orders got ___ on schedule.
questions.explanation: 'Got delivered' is a passive construction.

questions.stem: Commercial builders downplayed ___ a bust in the superheated housing market.
questions.explanation: 'Concerns about' is the correct noun phrase.

questions.stem: The executives pointed to immigration ___ the biggest drivers of the domestic market.
questions.explanation: 'As one of' introduces an example from a category.

questions.stem: The company expects to see ___ breakeven and a 15 cent a share loss in the second quarter.
questions.explanation: 'Somewhere between' indicates a range.

questions.stem: First quarter revenue ___ $45.1 billion from $44.7 billion a year earlier.
questions.explanation: 'Rose to' indicates an increase to a specific amount.

questions.stem: The report showed that overall prices are up 3.1 percent ___ 12 months.
questions.explanation: 'During the last' indicates a time period.

questions.stem: Property taxes ___ about 40 percent of the overall tax revenue the state collects.
questions.explanation: 'Account for' means to comprise or represent.

questions.stem: Strong exports ___ in driving first-quarter growth, rising 35 percent from a year earlier.
questions.explanation: 'Played a big role' is an idiomatic expression.

questions.stem: The new law will encourage growth in the export market ___.
questions.explanation: 'Next year' is a time expression that fits grammatically.

questions.stem: The new service was expected to be a success; ___ very few customers upgraded their accounts.
questions.explanation: 'Yet' shows contrast between expectation and reality.

questions.stem: I'd like to apologize for the noise on level 2 which is due to maintenance work ___ carried out on the premises.
questions.explanation: 'Being' forms present participle passive: 'being carried out'.

questions.stem: The secretary ___ the minutes during the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Took the minutes' is the correct collocation for recording meeting notes.

questions.stem: Everyone in the department is expected to ___ the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Attend' is the correct verb for being present at a meeting.

questions.stem: I regret to ___ you that your order will be delayed by 3 weeks.
questions.explanation: 'Inform' is the formal verb for giving information.

questions.stem: I have noticed that many members of staff are currently not ___ to the dress code guidelines laid out in the employee manual.
questions.explanation: 'Adhering to' means following or sticking to rules.

questions.stem: I'd like to show my ___ to all the staff who volunteered to help out at the charity evening.
questions.explanation: 'Appreciation' means gratitude or thankfulness.

questions.stem: We notice that payment for your electricity bill is overdue. Please note that ___ payment is not received within 7 days, your power will be cut off.
questions.explanation: 'If' introduces the conditional clause.

questions.stem: You will need to find out the weight and dimensions of your parcel in order to select a delivery service. ___ then can we give you an accurate quote.
questions.explanation: 'Only then' requires subject-verb inversion.

questions.stem: The administrative assistant should do a stock check every fortnight using form OS201 to check if we are ___ low on any supplies.
questions.explanation: 'Running low' means becoming short of supply.

questions.stem: In the ___ that you are injured at work, your medical insurance will provide you with private health care.
questions.explanation: 'In the event that' is formal language for 'if'.

questions.stem: The package ___ delivered yesterday.
questions.explanation: Passive voice with past time requires 'was'.

questions.stem: The committee ___ reached a decision yet.
questions.explanation: Present perfect negative with 'yet' requires 'hasn't'.

questions.stem: We need ___ volunteer to help with the event.
questions.explanation: Singular countable noun needs 'a' before consonant sound.

questions.stem: The manager will ___ the new guidelines at the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Outline' means to describe the main features.

questions.stem: The renovation project is ___ to be completed by March.
questions.explanation: 'Expected' indicates anticipation of completion.

questions.stem: Sales increased ___ during the holiday season.
questions.explanation: Adverb 'significantly' modifies the verb 'increased'.

questions.stem: This quarter's profits are ___ than last quarter's.
questions.explanation: Comparative form for one-syllable adjective: 'higher'.

questions.stem: If the shipment ___ on time, we wouldn't have lost the contract.
questions.explanation: Third conditional: 'had arrived' for past unreal situation.

questions.stem: The CEO's decision was made ___ of the board's recommendation.
questions.explanation: 'Regardless' means without being affected by.

questions.stem: The company decided ___ its headquarters to a new location.
questions.explanation: 'Decided to move' uses infinitive after 'decide'.

questions.stem: We appreciate ___ with us on this project.
questions.explanation: 'Appreciate' is followed by gerund: 'working'.

questions.stem: The meeting will ___ at 2 p.m. in the main conference room.
questions.explanation: 'Commence' means to begin.

questions.stem: The consultant ___ we hired last month has excellent credentials.
questions.explanation: 'Whom' is the object of 'hired' in the relative clause.

questions.stem: The report provides a ___ overview of the company's performance.
questions.explanation: 'Comprehensive' means complete and thorough.

questions.stem: Each of the employees ___ required to attend the training.
questions.explanation: 'Each' takes singular verb 'is'.

questions.stem: The new software will ___ improve our workflow efficiency.
questions.explanation: 'Substantially' means to a large degree.

questions.stem: The contract ___ by both parties before the end of the month.
questions.explanation: Future passive: 'will be signed'.

questions.stem: The employees who practiced excellent working habits were able to ___ the company management.
questions.explanation: 'Satisfy' is the verb form needed after 'to'.

questions.stem: The flight arrives ___ Tokyo in three hours.
questions.explanation: Use 'in' with cities when referring to arrival location.

questions.stem: He is excited about the new promotion and looking forward to ___ more responsibilities.
questions.explanation: 'Taking on' means to assume or accept (responsibilities).

questions.stem: My line manager wants the meeting ___ immediately.
questions.explanation: 'Want something done' uses past participle for passive meaning.

questions.stem: The software developers ___ investigated the latest problem.
questions.explanation: 'Have already' is the correct position for 'already' with present perfect.

questions.stem: While the stock ___ the staff worked in the evenings.
questions.explanation: 'Was being checked' is past continuous passive for ongoing action.

questions.stem: ___ the end of year results were published, the managers got their bonuses.
questions.explanation: 'When' indicates the timing of the bonus distribution.

questions.stem: All the orders got ___ on schedule.
questions.explanation: 'Got delivered' uses past participle in passive construction.

questions.stem: Commercial builders downplayed ___ a bust in the superheated housing market.
questions.explanation: 'Concerns about' is the correct collocation for expressing worries.

questions.stem: The executives pointed to immigration ___ the biggest drivers of the domestic market.
questions.explanation: 'As one of' correctly identifies immigration among multiple factors.

questions.stem: The company expects to see ___ breakeven and a 15 cent a share loss in the second quarter.
questions.explanation: 'Somewhere between' indicates a range between two values.

questions.stem: First quarter revenue ___ $45.1 billion from $44.7 billion a year earlier.
questions.explanation: 'Rose to' indicates an increase reaching a specific amount.

questions.stem: The report showed that overall prices are up 3.1 percent ___ 12 months.
questions.explanation: 'During the last' is correct for describing a period up to now.

questions.stem: Property taxes ___ about 40 percent of the overall tax revenue the state collects.
questions.explanation: 'Account for' means to make up or constitute a portion.

questions.stem: Strong exports ___ in driving first-quarter growth, rising 35 percent from a year earlier.
questions.explanation: 'Played a big role' is the idiom for having significant influence.

questions.stem: The new law will encourage growth in the export market ___.
questions.explanation: 'Next year' is a specific time adverbial for future actions.

questions.stem: The new service was expected to be a success; ___ very few customers upgraded their accounts.
questions.explanation: 'Yet' shows contrast between expectation and reality.

questions.stem: I'd like to apologize for the noise on level 2 which is due to maintenance work ___ carried out on the premises.
questions.explanation: 'Being' forms the present participle passive construction.

questions.stem: The secretary ___ the minutes during the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Take minutes' is the correct collocation for recording meeting notes.

questions.stem: Everyone in the department is expected to ___ the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Attend' is the formal verb for going to meetings or events.

questions.stem: I regret to ___ you that your order will be delayed by 3 weeks.
questions.explanation: 'Inform' is the formal verb for giving information in business contexts.

questions.stem: I have noticed that many members of staff are currently not ___ to the dress code guidelines.
questions.explanation: 'Adhering to' means following or obeying rules or guidelines.

questions.stem: I'd like to show my ___ to all the staff who volunteered to help out at the charity evening.
questions.explanation: 'Appreciation' expresses gratitude or thanks.

questions.stem: Please note that ___ payment is not received within 7 days, your power will be cut off.
questions.explanation: 'If' introduces the condition for the consequence.

questions.stem: ___ then can we give you an accurate quote.
questions.explanation: 'Only then' with inversion emphasizes the condition required.

questions.stem: The assistant should do a stock check every fortnight to check if we are ___ low on any supplies.
questions.explanation: 'Running low' means becoming short of supplies.

questions.stem: In the ___ that you are injured at work, your medical insurance will provide you with private health care.
questions.explanation: 'In the event that' is a formal way of saying 'if'.

questions.stem: Dear Team, We are pleased to announce that our annual conference will be held on June 15. ___
questions.explanation: Follow-up should provide relevant details about the event.

options.text: The weather has been nice lately.

questions.stem: Notice: The building will undergo maintenance this weekend. ___
questions.explanation: A notice should explain impact or alternative arrangements.

options.text: Please use the side entrance during this time.
The cafeteria serves lunch at noon.
The CEO will retire next year.

questions.stem: Thank you for your order. Your items have been shipped and should arrive within 5 business days. ___
questions.explanation: Order confirmation should provide tracking or follow-up info.

options.text: You can track your package using the link below.
Our store hours are 9 AM to 6 PM.
We hired ten new employees this month.

questions.stem: Memo: Effective immediately, all expense reports must include original receipts. ___
questions.explanation: Policy memos typically explain consequences or procedures.

options.text: Reports without receipts will not be processed.
The annual picnic was well attended.
Our competitor launched a new product.

questions.stem: We regret to inform you that the position has been filled. ___ We encourage you to apply for future openings.
questions.explanation: Rejection letters typically thank the applicant.

options.text: We appreciate your interest in our company.
The interview room is on the third floor.
Our CEO will speak at the conference.

questions.stem: To whom it may concern, With regards to the outstanding invoice, we have incurred additional costs. We wish you ___ us the payment immediately.
questions.explanation: The subjunctive form 'would send' is needed after 'wish' to express a desired action.

questions.stem: I am writing to request temporary staff to cover Rebecca's annual leave next month. Could you please get in touch ___ me to discuss arrangements?
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'get in touch with someone' to mean contact.

questions.stem: When writing a resume, include your qualifications, grades, employment history and the names of two ___.
questions.explanation: A 'referee' is a person who recommends you, not 'referral' which is the act of referring.

questions.stem: As a consultant, I can ___ you on how to improve your product design for $500 per day.
questions.explanation: Use 'advise someone on how' rather than 'recommend you on how'.

questions.stem: Would you mind ___ offices for the day? Our department needs a larger room for a meeting.
questions.explanation: After 'mind' use the gerund (-ing) form, so 'swapping' is correct.

questions.stem: Ice cream sales boom in summer but plummet in winter. In autumn, sales ___ gradually as temperatures drop.
questions.explanation: 'Rise' is intransitive (no object), while 'raise' is transitive. Sales rise by themselves.

questions.stem: Please make some alterations to Rachael's report. Her name is spelled incorrectly and several figures need to be ___.
questions.explanation: 'Transform' means complete change; 'amended' or 'adjusted' fits minor corrections.

questions.stem: Due to difficult economic conditions, it is not ___ to recruit more staff this year.
questions.explanation: 'Advisable' means wise or recommended; 'advisory' describes advisors.

questions.stem: I have noted staff ___ about converting the common room into an additional office space.
questions.explanation: 'Objections' means opposition or concerns; easily confused with 'objectives' which means goals.

questions.stem: Our factory requires certification and strict quality control. Any ___ that fail inspection will be rejected.
questions.explanation: 'Products' refers to manufactured items; 'produce' refers to crops/vegetables.

questions.stem: The economy is ___ dependent on industry, and growth has always been of greater concern than environmental preservation.
questions.explanation: An adverb ('heavily') is required to modify the adjective 'dependent'.

questions.stem: Advocacy for child welfare has attracted the attention of legislators and policy-makers ___ the world.
questions.explanation: 'Throughout' means in every part of; 'through' means moving in one side and out the other.

questions.stem: The minimum wage bill provides for a gradual ___ of the minimum wage from $5.15 to $7.25 an hour.
questions.explanation: An 'increase' describes going from a lower to higher amount; 'reduction' would be the opposite.

questions.stem: The technology involves partly burning coal to turn it into a gas, then using a catalyst ___ it a liquid.
questions.explanation: The infinitive 'to make' is required for the parallel structure.

questions.stem: In 2004, a team led by the environment ministry ___ that chemicals from the factory had contaminated sediment.
questions.explanation: Past tense 'said' is needed to match the past context (2004).

questions.stem: Email: 'Dear valued customer, We are writing to inform you that our store location at 45 Main Street will be permanently closing on March 31.' What is the purpose of this email?
questions.explanation: The email announces a store closure.

options.text: To notify customers of a store closure

questions.stem: Notice: 'All employees must complete the online safety training by October 15.' What is required of employees?
questions.explanation: The notice specifies completing training by a deadline.

options.text: To finish safety training before the deadline
To schedule a meeting with HR

questions.stem: Article: 'Despite initial delays, the new metro line opened to the public last Tuesday, three months behind schedule.' What can be inferred?
questions.explanation: The article implies the original opening was planned earlier.

options.text: The metro was originally scheduled to open earlier
The metro only runs on Tuesdays

questions.stem: Memo: 'Employees who wish to participate in the wellness program should register by November 1. Participants will receive a 10% discount on gym memberships.' What benefit is offered?
questions.explanation: The memo mentions a discount on gym memberships.

questions.stem: Letter: 'I am writing to inquire about the availability of conference rooms for our upcoming training session on April 5.' What is the purpose of this letter?
questions.explanation: The sender is requesting information about room availability.

questions.stem: Article: 'The acquisition was finalized last week, making GlobalTech the largest tech company in the region.' What does 'acquisition' most likely mean?
questions.explanation: 'Acquisition' refers to purchasing or taking over a company.

options.text: The launch of a new product
The opening of a new office

questions.stem: Email exchange: Person A: 'The client wants the proposal by Friday.' Person B: 'I can have it ready by Thursday evening.' What will Person B do?
questions.explanation: Person B commits to finishing the proposal by Thursday evening.

options.text: Complete the proposal before the deadline

questions.stem: Review: 'While the hotel's location was excellent, the room cleanliness left much to be desired.' What does the reviewer imply?
questions.explanation: The reviewer was dissatisfied with the room cleanliness.

options.text: The room was not clean enough
The location was difficult to find

questions.stem: Customer reviews indicate that many modern mobile devices are often unnecessarily ___.
questions.explanation: ETS Official - 'complicated' is the adjective form needed after the adverb 'unnecessarily'.

questions.stem: Jamal Nawzad has received top performance reviews ___ he joined the sales department two years ago.
questions.explanation: ETS Official - 'since' indicates the starting point of a time period.

questions.stem: Gyeon Corporation's continuing education policy states that ___ learning new skills enhances creativity and focus.
questions.explanation: ETS Official - 'regularly' is the adverb form needed to modify the gerund 'learning'.

questions.stem: Among ___ recognized at the company awards ceremony were senior business analyst Natalie Obi and sales associate Peter Comeau.
questions.explanation: ETS Official - 'those' is the demonstrative pronoun referring to people.

questions.stem: All clothing sold in Develyn's Boutique is made from natural materials and contains no ___ dyes.
questions.explanation: ETS Official - 'synthetic' means artificial or man-made.

questions.stem: Passage: 'Are you setting up a small business? Worried about the costs of renting office space and employing the right people? Rebus Virtual Office World can help you...' Where is the text from?
questions.explanation: The text promotes a service for small businesses - this is an advertisement.

options.text: A message from a business to a current client
An advertisement for a new business service
An email from one business worker to another
A newspaper article about a new business's success

questions.stem: Passage about Rebus Virtual Office World: What does the service provide?
questions.explanation: Virtual office services provide remote staff for office duties.

options.text: Off-site staff to perform general office duties
A site where several businesses can locate their offices
Advice on how to make your business more professional

questions.stem: Passage about Rebus Virtual Office World Basic Office deal: Which of the following is NOT included?
questions.explanation: Identify what is excluded from the basic package.

questions.stem: Letter: 'Dear Helen, I would like to congratulate you on organising such an excellent and informative workshop...' What is the main objective of the message?
questions.explanation: The letter begins with congratulations - its purpose is to praise.

questions.stem: Workshop congratulations letter: What can be implied about the workshop?
questions.explanation: Context clues suggest the workshop was a single-day event.

options.text: All the delegates were staff from the same office
Motivation was the only topic discussed

questions.stem: Workshop letter mentioning Dr Friedman: What can be implied about Dr Friedman?
questions.explanation: The context suggests Dr Friedman has many commitments.

options.text: He works in the same office as Anne
He has a very busy schedule
He is a leading expert on staff motivation
Anne knows him better than Helen does

questions.stem: Workshop letter about an address list: What has happened to the address list?
questions.explanation: The letter mentions the list being misplaced.

options.text: Anne has sent it to Helen

questions.stem: Cookie recipe passage: Which of the following is NOT an ingredient in the recipe?
questions.explanation: Identify ingredients not mentioned in the recipe.

questions.stem: Cookie recipe passage: What must the cook do first?
questions.explanation: Recipes typically start with preheating the oven.

options.text: Put the margarine and sugar in a bowl

questions.stem: Cookie recipe passage: Which of the following items is needed to make this recipe?
questions.explanation: A sieve is used for sifting flour.

questions.stem: Cookie recipe passage: Which of the following ingredients is added last?
questions.explanation: Chocolate chips are typically folded in at the end.

questions.stem: Fire alarm ordinance passage: Which of the following is true about the new rule?
questions.explanation: The new rule sets quality standards for fire alarms.

options.text: Every house should have five fire alarms
All fire alarms must be of a certain quality
All fire alarms need to first be approved by the Fire Department
All fire alarms must be less than six months old

questions.stem: Fire alarm ordinance passage: How much do five fire alarms cost?
questions.explanation: Calculate total cost based on given unit price.

questions.stem: Fire alarm ordinance passage: Which of the following people may be able to receive a free fire alarm?
questions.explanation: Elderly residents often qualify for free safety equipment.

options.text: a family of two adults and two teenagers
a man who owns his own home

questions.stem: Fire alarm ordinance passage: What does the number 5,000 refer to?
questions.explanation: The number refers to available free alarms for eligible residents.

options.text: the number of people eligible for free smoke alarms
the number of smoke alarms needing replacement in McKinley
the number of smoke alarms already installed by Fire Department
the number of smoke alarms available free to certain people

questions.stem: Fire alarm ordinance passage about Gwen Ellis: Why did Gwen Ellis have to buy new smoke alarms?
questions.explanation: Her previous alarms didn't meet new safety standards.

options.text: she now has a young family
her previous alarms didn't meet the safety regulations
she has just moved into a new house

questions.stem: Street closure notice: 'Dear Residents, Due to the Fitness First cycling event on Sunday 14th March, this street will be closed to traffic...' Who is the letter to?
questions.explanation: The notice addresses residents who park on the affected street.

options.text: People who are attending the cycling event
Car owners living on one street

questions.stem: Street closure notice: What must residents do?
questions.explanation: Residents must relocate their vehicles during the event.

options.text: Co-operate with the cyclists at the event

questions.stem: Stress prevention article: 'Do you feel stressed? Chances are, you do. Levels of stress in our society are increasing more and more...' What is the text about?
questions.explanation: The article discusses ways to prevent stress.

options.text: Reasons why stress levels are increasing
The consequences of a stressful lifestyle

questions.stem: Stress prevention article: Why does the writer consider commercials stressful?
questions.explanation: Commercials create unrealistic expectations about life.

options.text: They often depict characters in stressful situations
They make people worry about money
They make you feel your life is not good enough
They contain flashing and fast-moving images

questions.stem: Stress prevention article: Why, according to the writer, do news stories often depress us?
questions.explanation: News often covers problems we can't personally solve.

options.text: The stories highlight problems in the local neighbourhood
The stories address problems that the reader cannot control
The stories describe people who are powerless
The stories force readers to take action

questions.stem: Stress prevention article: What does the writer suggest you do if you feel angry with someone?
questions.explanation: Taking time alone helps manage anger.

questions.stem: Stress prevention article: Which drink does the writer recommend when you feel stressed?
questions.explanation: Herbal tea is recommended for stress relief.

questions.stem: GDP economic report: 'The country's GDP increased by 0.9 per cent in the third quarter of 2009; significantly higher than the rate of 0.4 percent seen in the previous quarter...' Which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: GDP increased in both quarters mentioned.

options.text: It increased in both the second and third quarters

questions.stem: The manager ___ the report by the time you arrive.
questions.explanation: Future perfect tense for completed action before another future event.

questions.stem: The company ___ its headquarters to Shanghai last year.
questions.explanation: Past simple for completed action in the past.

questions.stem: By next December, we ___ in this office for ten years.
questions.explanation: Future perfect continuous for duration up to a future point.

questions.stem: If the package ___ on time, please notify the customer.
questions.explanation: First conditional with present tense in if-clause.

questions.stem: If I ___ about the delay, I would have rescheduled the meeting.
questions.explanation: Third conditional requires past perfect in if-clause.

questions.stem: She is ___ excellent manager with years of experience.
questions.explanation: Use "an" before vowel sounds.

questions.stem: ___ meeting scheduled for tomorrow has been postponed.
questions.explanation: Use "the" for specific, known items.

questions.stem: Neither the manager nor the employees ___ satisfied with the proposal.
questions.explanation: With neither...nor, verb agrees with nearest subject.

questions.stem: Each of the employees ___ required to attend the training.
questions.explanation: Each is singular, requires singular verb.

questions.stem: The sales team exceeded their quarterly ___ by 15 percent.
questions.explanation: Quota means a set target or goal.

questions.stem: The new policy will take ___ starting next month.
questions.explanation: Take effect means to become active.

questions.stem: The company is seeking ___ investors to fund the expansion.
questions.explanation: Prospective means potential or likely.

questions.stem: Please ___ your request in writing to the HR department.
questions.explanation: Submit means to formally present for consideration.

questions.stem: The ___ of the new factory will create 500 jobs.
questions.explanation: Construction is the noun form needed here.

questions.stem: Her presentation was ___ prepared and well received.
questions.explanation: Thoroughly is the adverb form needed to modify "prepared".

questions.stem: The ___ of online shopping has changed retail forever.
questions.explanation: Popularity is the noun form needed after "the".

questions.stem: The conference will be held ___ March 15th.
questions.explanation: Use "on" with specific dates.

questions.stem: We are looking forward ___ your reply.
questions.explanation: Look forward to requires "to".

questions.stem: The office is located ___ the corner of Main Street and Oak Avenue.
questions.explanation: Use "at" for a specific point/corner.

questions.stem: Dear Mr. Thompson, Thank you for your interest in our services. ___ We will contact you within 48 hours to schedule a consultation.
questions.explanation: A transition sentence about next steps fits the context.

options.text: Our office will be closed tomorrow.

questions.stem: All employees must complete the safety training by December 1st. ___ Failure to complete the training may result in disciplinary action.
questions.explanation: A warning or additional requirement fits the formal notice context.

options.text: The cafeteria serves lunch from noon to 2 p.m.

questions.stem: The quarterly report shows a 12% increase in revenue. ___ However, operating costs have also risen during this period.
questions.explanation: A positive statement about growth fits before the "However".

options.text: This exceeds our projected growth target.
Please contact HR for more information.
The office will be renovated next month.

questions.stem: We apologize for any inconvenience caused by the system outage. ___ Thank you for your patience.
questions.explanation: An update about resolution fits between apology and thanks.

options.text: Our technicians are working to resolve the issue.
The company picnic is next Friday.
The CEO will be on vacation.

questions.stem: Effective immediately, all visitors must sign in at the front desk. ___ This policy is intended to enhance building security.
questions.explanation: Additional requirements or exceptions complement the new rule.

options.text: Employees are exempt from this requirement.
We hope you enjoy your stay.

To advertise a new parking garage

questions.stem: Email: "The client meeting originally scheduled for Tuesday has been moved to Thursday at 2:00 PM. Please confirm your availability by end of day today." When is the new meeting time?
questions.explanation: The email states the new time is Thursday at 2:00 PM.

questions.stem: Memo: "Due to budget constraints, the annual team building retreat will be held locally this year instead of at the mountain resort. Activities will include workshops and team exercises." What can be inferred about previous retreats?
questions.explanation: The contrast with "locally this year" implies previous retreats were held at more distant locations.

options.text: They were held at more expensive locations

questions.stem: Advertisement: "Grand Opening Sale! Save 20% on all electronics this weekend only. Store hours: Saturday 9 AM - 9 PM, Sunday 10 AM - 6 PM. Free delivery on orders over $100." What is the minimum purchase for free delivery?
questions.explanation: The ad states free delivery on orders over $100.

questions.stem: Notice: "Reminder: All expense reports for Q4 must be submitted by December 15th. Late submissions will be processed in the following quarter. Questions? Contact accounting@company.com." Why was this notice written?
questions.explanation: The notice reminds employees of the expense report deadline.

options.text: To announce a new expense policy
To remind about a submission deadline

questions.stem: Letter: "We regret to inform you that your application for the Marketing Manager position was not successful. However, we were impressed with your qualifications and would like to keep your resume on file for future opportunities." What does this letter imply?
questions.explanation: Keeping resume on file suggests future consideration.

options.text: The applicant should not apply again
The applicant may be considered for other positions

questions.stem: The employee ___ performance was outstanding received a bonus.
questions.explanation: Whose shows possession for people.

questions.stem: The report ___ you requested is now ready.
questions.explanation: Which/that for things; can be omitted in defining clauses.

questions.stem: This quarter's sales are ___ than last quarter's.
questions.explanation: Use "higher" for comparison of two items.

questions.stem: Of all the candidates, she has the ___ experience.
questions.explanation: Use superlative "most" when comparing three or more.

questions.stem: The new policy ___ by the board last week.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + past participle.

questions.stem: The project ___ by the end of next month.
questions.explanation: Future passive: will be + past participle.

questions.stem: Employees ___ wear ID badges at all times.
questions.explanation: Must expresses obligation/requirement.

questions.stem: You ___ submit the form online or by mail.
questions.explanation: May expresses permission/possibility.

questions.stem: The meeting was ___ due to the CEO's illness.
questions.explanation: Postponed means moved to a later time.

questions.stem: The company's ___ policy allows employees to work from home.
questions.explanation: Flexible means adaptable or adjustable.

questions.stem: We need to ___ the project timeline to meet the deadline.
questions.explanation: Accelerate means to speed up.

questions.stem: Job Posting: "Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Business or related field, 3+ years of experience in sales, excellent communication skills. Salary: Competitive, based on experience." What is a requirement for this position?
questions.explanation: The posting lists 3+ years of sales experience as a requirement.

questions.stem: Article: "The company announced record profits for the third consecutive quarter. CEO Smith attributed the success to increased online sales and cost-cutting measures implemented last year." What is the main topic?
questions.explanation: The article is about the company's financial success.

questions.stem: Review: "The hotel's location is excellent, just steps from the subway. However, the rooms could use updating and the breakfast options were limited." What does the reviewer imply?
questions.explanation: The reviewer suggests the hotel has both advantages and disadvantages.

options.text: The hotel has both strengths and weaknesses

questions.stem: Email: "Thank you for your order. Your items will be shipped within 2-3 business days. You will receive a tracking number once your package is on its way. For questions, contact support@shop.com." What is the purpose of this email?
questions.explanation: The email confirms the order and provides shipping information.

questions.stem: Schedule: "Training Session - Room 201, 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM. Lunch Break - Cafeteria, 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM. Workshop - Room 305, 1:00 PM - 4:00 PM." Where will the afternoon workshop be held?
questions.explanation: The schedule shows the workshop is in Room 305.

questions.stem: The committee discussed ___ the annual budget.
questions.explanation: Discuss is followed by a gerund.

questions.stem: The company decided ___ its operations overseas.
questions.explanation: Decide is followed by an infinitive.

questions.stem: The ___ of the contract requires approval from legal.
questions.explanation: Amendment means a change or modification.

questions.stem: The proposal was rejected due to ___ budget concerns.
questions.explanation: Budgetary is the adjective form of budget.

questions.stem: The training program consists ___ three modules.
questions.explanation: Consist of is the correct collocation.

questions.stem: She was promoted ___ senior manager last month.
questions.explanation: Promote to indicates the new position.

questions.stem: The company will ___ a new branch in Tokyo.
questions.explanation: Establish means to set up or create.

questions.stem: The CEO gave a ___ speech about the company vision.
questions.explanation: Compelling means convincing and engaging.
